14.reason: Error: Using %d to read a char causes undefined behavior; should be %c. This leads to incorrect input parsing and potential runtime errors.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  Error: Using %d to read a char causes undefined behavior; should be %c. This leads to incorrect input parsing and potential runtime errors.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  Error: Using %d to read a char causes undefined behavior; should be %c. This leads to incorrect reading of the newline character, causing the loop to not terminate properly.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: Using %d format specifier to read a char can cause buffer overflow; should use %c]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: should use %c to read a character, not %d]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The format specifier is "%d" but the variable c0 is of type char. This causes scanf to write 4 bytes (size of int) into a 1-byte variable, leading to stack corruption (stack smashing). It should be " %c" or similar to read the separator character correctly.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: Format specifier mismatch. scanf expects an int pointer for "%d", but &c0 is a char pointer. This causes a buffer overflow/stack corruption because scanf will write 4 bytes (size of int) into a 1-byte variable (char), leading to "stack smashing detected". It should be " %c" or similar to read the separator character correctly.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The format specifier "%d" is used to read an integer, but c0 is of type char. This causes scanf to write 4 (or more) bytes into a 1-byte variable, corrupting adjacent stack memory and leading to stack smashing. It should be scanf("%c", &c0);]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The format specifier "%d" is used to read an integer, but c0 is of type char. This causes scanf to write 4 (or more) bytes into a 1-byte variable, leading to stack corruption. It should be scanf(" %c", &c0) or at least scanf("%c", &c0).]
